Testing affect regulation via computational modeling of real-time alcohol choices in daily life: Boundary Separation (B)

2026-04-06

1 About This Report

This report presents results for Boundary Separation (B) (B) across all four focal predictors: stress (binary), stress (state), negative affect (state), and positive affect (state).

For each predictor, the report is organized as follows:

  • Dimensionality reduction: PCA and UMAP component composition tables, showing which raw variables load most strongly onto each component.
  • GLMM results (raw features): Summary of results from GLMM model with raw variables.
  • Random forest results (raw features): Summary of results from random forest model with raw variables.
  • Model comparison: RMSE comparison table across all methods (GLMM, random forest, decision tree) and feature sets (raw, PCA, UMAP).

Method Raw PCA UMAP
glmm 0.3442 0.3444 0.3441
forest 0.3159 0.3355 0.3230
tree 0.3449 0.3449 0.3449

Note: The MetaForest package (metaforest) uses random forests designed for meta-analytic data, where observations have known sampling variances and are nested within studies. While the clustering structure (observations within participants) is conceptually similar to EMA data, MetaForest specifically requires sampling variance estimates (vi) that are not available in this context. The analytical patterns from that framework (variable importance, partial dependence) have been adopted above without the MetaForest package itself.
